chunks.title: c01
chunks.text:
DRAFT ENVIRONMENTAL IMPACT STATEMENT

Table of Contents

1. Purpose and Need ..... 2
2. Alternatives ......... 5
3. Affected Environment . 8

 1. PURPOSE AND NEED

The proposed Highway 99 expansion project is needed to address severe traffic congestion
in the Cedar Valley region of Oregon. The Federal Highway Administration proposes to widen
Highway 99 from two lanes to four lanes over a distance of 12 miles.

The project area includes Cedar Valley, Lane County, Oregon.

 2. ALTERNATIVES

Alternative A — No Action: Continue current two-lane configuration. Traffic congestion
would continue to worsen. No environmental impacts from construction.

Alternative B — Preferred Action: Widen to four lanes along existing alignment.
Moderate wetland impacts. Displaces 12 households.

Alternative C — Northern Bypass: New four-lane road north of existing alignment.
Greater wetland impacts. Displaces fewer households.
